macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Trump Family Revealed to Earn Over $1.4 Billion from Crypto Ventures in 2025
items.summary: President Donald Trump's mandatory 2025 financial disclosure shows his family earned more than $1.4 billion from cryptocurrency businesses, including $635 million in meme coin royalties and $500 million from World Liberty Financial token sales, though both assets have since plunged in value.

items.title: Trump Defends $1.2B Crypto Profit Amid Conflict-of-Interest Concerns
items.summary: President Trump defended his family's $1.2 billion crypto earnings from 2025, stating 'everybody's profiting,' while critics highlight his rollback of Biden-era crypto regulations and appointment of Paul Atkins as SEC chair to oversee financial dealings.

items.title: Trump Meme Coins and World Liberty Financial Tokens Plunge in Price
items.summary: Despite generating over $1 billion in revenue for the Trump family, both the Trump meme coins and World Liberty Financial governance tokens have crashed in value since their sale, leaving small investors out of pocket.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 38% Yearly, Outperforming Gold and S&P 500
items.summary: Bitcoin has gained 38% over the past year, significantly outperforming gold and the S&P 500, with Strategy (now counting Trump as a shareholder) offering new investment access for savers and pensioners.

items.title: Arthur Hayes Pardoned by Trump in 2025, Demands Further Crypto Deregulation
items.summary: BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes, who received a presidential pardon from Donald Trump in 2025, is now demanding additional market deregulation to unleash the full potential of cryptocurrency technology.

items.title: Crypto Becomes Largest Corporate Funding Source in 2024 Election
items.summary: Cryptocurrency emerged as the biggest corporate funding pot in the 2024 U.S. election, with the Trump family actively evangelizing its value and securing historic influence in the White House through crypto-friendly policymakers.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Officially Reboots Claude Fable 5 After U.S. Export Restrictions Lifted
items.summary: After nearly three weeks offline, Claude Fable 5 is back, delivering the highest quality across all scenes at a cost of $3.12 for 62,158 tokens, marking a major shift in AI availability following the lifting of U.S. export restrictions.

items.title: Google DeepMind Releases Nano Banana 2 Lite and New AI Video/Image Models
items.summary: Google DeepMind launched Nano Banana 2 Lite, a model capable of training a small chat model in under five minutes to optimize bits-per-byte, alongside new AI video and image generation models that enhance creative workflows.

items.title: ByteDance Preparing Seedance 2.5 Video Model with 30-Second Native Generation
items.summary: ByteDance is reportedly launching Seedance 2.5 in China within two weeks, featuring 30-second native single-shot video generation, higher reference material capacity, and improved controllability for video creation.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 5 Demonstrates Autonomous 3D Animation via Blender
items.summary: Claude Sonnet 5 autonomously planned, modeled, simulated, and rendered a 3D animation in Blender for nearly two hours, showcasing AI agents evolving into autonomous creative systems capable of complex workflows with minimal human intervention.

items.title: xAI Launches Grok Voice Agent Builder for Custom Voice AI Systems
items.summary: xAI introduced Grok's Voice Agent Builder, enabling users to create custom voice agent systems, while also revealing GPT-5.6 Codex leaks with a new context window update that enhances large language model capabilities.

items.title: Z.ai Releases ZCode 3.0 Coding IDE with Enhanced AI Integration
items.summary: Z.ai launched ZCode 3.0, a new coding IDE featuring advanced AI integration for developers, complementing broader industry trends toward AI-powered development tools and autonomous coding agents.

items.title: Figure AI Advances Humanoid Robot Development with Figure 03 Updates
items.summary: Figure AI announced significant updates to its Figure 03 humanoid robot, advancing the field of embodied AI and autonomous physical agents capable of interacting with real-world environments.

items.title: Micron Market Cap Surges Past Meta and Tesla on AI-Driven Memory Demand
items.summary: Memory chip maker Micron briefly surpassed Meta and Tesla in market cap, hitting nearly $1.27 trillion due to extraordinary demand for memory and storage driven by rapid AI data center expansion.

items.title: ARC-AGI-3 Competition Highlights LLM Limitations in Agentic Goal Discovery
items.summary: The ARC-AGI-3 competition, which makes ARC interactive and agentic, continues to challenge LLMs by requiring models to discover goals rather than transduce static grids, with researchers noting more base model research is needed before November.

items.title: AI Agents Now Build Most of User's AI Memory Stack via Claude or Codex
items.summary: Recent breakthroughs show AI agents can autonomously build most of a user's AI memory stack by simply interacting with Claude or Codex, shifting industry focus toward owning memory infrastructure while renting intelligence.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Veterans: Napoleonic Wars Reinstated on Steam After DMCA Counter-Notice
items.summary: The game Veterans: Napoleonic Wars has returned to Steam after 12 business days, as DMCA regulations required reinstatement when no federal lawsuit was filed within the 10–14 business day window following the developers' counter-notice.

items.title: Sony Announces New PlayStation Controller with Unexpected Design at State of Play
items.summary: During Sony’s June State of Play showcase, the company revealed a new PlayStation controller featuring an unexpected design, alongside announcements for Ninja Gaiden, Nioh 3, and a new Bloodstained entry.

items.title: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo Announce Nioh 3 at Sony State of Play
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo unveiled Nioh 3 during Sony’s June State of Play, offering immediate access for soulslike fans following the game's announcement.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals New Force of Law Warbond for Cooperative Shooter
items.summary: Helldivers 2 surprised fans by announcing the upcoming Force of Law Warbond, a new expansion for the cooperative shooter that will soon be available to players.

items.title: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture Unveil New Over-the-Top Action Game
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture revealed their latest action game, described as a fusion of No More Heroes and Dead Rising, marking a bold new entry in their signature style.

items.title: XDefiant Executive Producer Steps Away from Gaming Industry After One Year
items.summary: As XDefiant concludes its one-year run, the game’s executive producer announced he is leaving the gaming industry permanently, signaling a major shift in the franchise’s leadership.

items.title: Pokemon GO Announces Community Day Voting for August 2025 Edition
items.summary: Pokemon GO has reopened Community Day voting for its August 2025 event, with four Pokemon candidates selected to be the spotlight for the upcoming celebration.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: FCC Accelerates Approvals for Next-Gen Satellite Broadband Launches
items.summary: The FCC is implementing a 'licensing assembly line' to speed up approvals for new satellite broadband systems, aiming to unleash next-gen infrastructure faster despite concerns about orbital congestion.

items.title: Blue Origin Plans Simpler Infrastructure to Return New Glenn to Flight in 2026
items.summary: Following a May launchpad explosion, Blue Origin is adopting a simpler launch infrastructure to return its New Glenn rocket to flight faster, targeting a 2026 comeback despite an unprecedented work pace.

items.title: Microsoft Extends Free Security Updates for Windows 10 Through 2027
items.summary: Microsoft quietly extended free security updates for Windows 10 through 2027, giving millions of aging PCs another lease on life and potentially setting the stage for a longer reprieve.

items.title: Spotify Launches Free Streaming Option in South Korea
items.summary: Spotify has introduced a free streaming tier in South Korea, expanding its accessibility in the region as part of its global growth strategy.

items.title: Form Energy Secures $405M for 100-Hour Long-Duration Battery Development
items.summary: Form Energy, a battery maker producing alternatives to lithium batteries, secured $405 million in funding to accelerate the development of its long-awaited 100-hour battery technology.

items.title: Elon Musk's X Platform Returns to Brazil After Compliance with Judicial Demands
items.summary: The social media platform X is back in Brazil after being suspended for over a month, having complied with all judicial demands following a clash between Elon Musk and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice.

items.title: Apple Negotiates to Buy Chips from Chinese Semiconductor Firms on Pentagon Blacklist
items.summary: Apple is in negotiations to purchase chips from two Chinese semiconductor firms currently on the Pentagon blacklist, with CEO Tim Cook reportedly appealing to the Trump administration to mitigate political fallout.

items.title: Bending Spoons Executes $1.7 Billion IPO on Nasdaq
items.summary: Milan-based tech firm Bending Spoons, owner of AOL, has gone public on the Nasdaq with a $1.7 billion IPO, targeting a valuation of up to $20 billion.

items.title: SpaceX Adds Starlink Outage Register for Australia to Comply with New Regulations
items.summary: SpaceX has launched an Outage Register on its website for Australia, offering a downloadable spreadsheet with downtime data to comply with new transparency regulations.

items.title: US Water Utility Cyberattack Highlights Critical Infrastructure Vulnerabilities
items.summary: A continuing cyberattack on the nation's largest regulated water and wastewater utility has renewed focus on the urgent need to protect critical infrastructure from digital threats.
